name: Asana API
what_it_does: Asana's API for programmatically managing tasks, projects, teams, goals, and workflows in the Asana work management platform.
use_cases: Creating and updating tasks from agent workflows and integrations
Automating project creation and team assignment
Reading task status for status reporting and escalation
Building integrations that sync external events to Asana tasks
Automating recurring task creation and project templating
not_for: Real-time task collaboration or commenting at high frequency
Complex sprint planning with Agile-specific features (use Jira)
best_when: An agent needs to manage Asana tasks and projects as part of a work management automation workflow.
avoid_when: Your team uses a different project management tool, or you need Agile-specific sprint/backlog management.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens for single-user agent use. OAuth2 for multi-user apps with scope-based permissions. PATs are simplest for agents. Workspace-scoped access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 10 users, basic features, API access
pricing.notes: API access on all plans. Free tier has limited features. Advanced plan needed for goals, portfolios, and advanced workflow automation.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include errors array with message and help URL. Field-level validation errors are specific. Rate limit errors include Retry-After header.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Task creation will create duplicates on retry. Agents should use external_id field for deduplication where available.
known_agent_gotchas: External ID field exists on tasks but requires explicit setting — useful for idempotent agent operations
Webhook events require challenge verification during registration
Free tier rate limit (150 req/min) is very restrictive
Task dependencies and subtasks require separate API calls
Project sections must be created separately from tasks
Asana's data model is workspace-centric — always need workspace GID in requests
